revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24755395338|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Imaki-Aosaka-Inami-Ikegami-jinja (Kamikawa, Saitama)]][[ja:今城青坂稲実池上神社 (神川町関口)]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Imaki-Aosaka-Inami-Ikegami Shrine (Q72728268) | native_name = 今城青坂稲実池上神社 | image = IwakiAosakaInamiIkegami-jinja(Kanna-machi) Haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Imaki-Aosaka-Inami-Ikegami Shrine (Q72728268) | coordinates = {{coord|36.2215|139.09980555555555}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | established = {'time': '+0724-00-00T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 9, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985786'} }} {{nihongo|'''Imaki-Aosaka-Inami-Ikegami Shrine'''|今城青坂稲実池上神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11546334|ltq=Q11546334|qid=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 37 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039747|ltq=Q135039747|qid=Q135039747|1=Imakiawosakanoinamiaramitamano Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|lt=Chichibu Shrine|en|Chichibu Shrine|fr|Chichibu-jinja|ja|秩父神社|qid=Q2963366|1=Chichibu Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q1346757|ltq=Q1346757|qid=Q1346757|1=Kamikawa, Saitama}} * {{ill|Q907398|ltq=Q907398|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} * {{ill|Kami district|lt=Kami district|ja|賀美郡|ko|가미군 (사이타마현)|qid=Q11634927|1=Kami district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|36.2215|139.09980555555555}} * {{coord|36.221543|139.099987}} == inception (P571) == * {'time': '+0724-00-00T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 9, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985786'} == image (P18) == * IwakiAosakaInamiIkegami-jinja(Kanna-machi) Haiden.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181725 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917284|ltq=Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * Dineʼé Bikéyah|ny|Japan|oc|Japon|olo|Japounii|om|Jaappaan|or|ଜାପାନ|os|Япон|pag|Hapon|pam|Hapon|pap|Hapon|pa|ਜਪਾਨ|pcd|Japon|pcm|Japan|pdc|Japan|pfl|Japan|pl|Japonia|plnews|Kategoria:Japonia|plquote|Japonia|plvoyage|Japonia|pms|Giapon|pnb|جاپان|pnt|Ιαπωνία|ps|جاپان|pt|Japão|ptnews|Categoria:Japão|ptquote|Japão|ptvoyage|Japão|pwn|Dripun|qu|Hapun|rm|Giapun|rmy|Japan|rn|Yapon|roa_rup|Japonia|roa_tara|Giappone|ro|Japonia|ronews|Categorie:Japonia|rovoyage|Japonia|rue|Японія|ru|Япония|runews|Категория:Япония|ruvoyage|Япония|rw|Ubuyapani|sah|Дьоппуон|sat|ᱡᱟᱯᱟᱱ|sa|जपान्|scn|Giappuni|sco|Japan|sc|Giapone|sd|جاپان|se|Jáhpan|sg|Zapöo|shn|မိူင်းၵျႃႇပၢၼ်ႇ|sh|Japan|simple|Japan|si|ජපානය|skr|جاپان|sk|Japonsko|sl|Japonska|smn|Jaapaan|sm|Iapani|sn|Japani|so|Jabaan|sq|Japonia|sqnews|Kategoria:Japoni|srn|Japan|sr|Јапан|srnews|Категорија:Јапан|srquote|Јапан|ss|IJapani|stq|Japan|st|Japane|su|Jepang|sv|Japan|svnews|Kategori:Japan|svvoyage|Japan|sw|Japani|syl|ꠎꠣꠙꠣꠘ|szl|Japůńijo|szy|Zipun|ta|யப்பான்|tanews|பகுப்பு:ஜப்பான்|tay|Gipun|tcy|ಜಪಾನ್|tdd|ᥛᥫᥒᥰ ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いまきあおやさかいなみいけがみじんじゃ * -イケノウヘノEngishiki Jinmyōchō == postal code (P281) == * 367-0246https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5030005009203 == Commons category (P373) == * Imaki-Aosaka-Inami-Ikegami-jinja (Kamikawa, Saitama) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039748|ltq=Q135039748|qid=Q135039748|1=Imakiawosakainaminoikekamino Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '埼玉県児玉郡神川町関口38', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '埼玉県児玉郡上里町忍保', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Imaki-Aosaka-Inami-Ikegami-jinja (Kamikawa, Saitama)}} [[JA:今城青坂稲実池上神社 (神川町関口)]] {{wikidata link|Q72728268}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Imaki Aosaka Inami Ikegami Shrine |Image = [[File:IwakiAosakaInamiIkegami-jinja(Kanna-machi) Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|qid=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|qid=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}{{ill|lt=Kamikawa Town|Q1346757|qid=Q1346757|1=Kamikawa, Saitama}} Sekiguchi 38 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okami no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime|Q2983724|qid=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime no Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Haniyasuhime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyoukehime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shintai = |Rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = {{ill|lt=Jinki era|Q1373210|qid=Q1373210|1=Jinki (era)}} Year 1 ({{ill|lt=724|Q23549|qid=Q23549|1=724}}) February |Head priest = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = Niu-sama |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = October 19 |Rites = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Imaki Aosaka Inami Ikegami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Imakiaoyasakainamiikegami-jinja(Kamikawa) Hengaku.jpg|265px|thumb|{{center|Dedication plaque of the worship hall}}]] '''Imaki Aosaka Inami Ikegami Shrine''' (いまきあおさかいなみいけがみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone River system)|Q11590491|qid=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River|1=Kanna River}}, a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Tone River|Q177452|qid=Q177452|1=Tone River}}, in Sekiguchi, {{ill|lt=Kamikawa Town|Q1346757|qid=Q1346757|1=Kamikawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|qid=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|qid=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. The enshrined deities are Okami no Kami, Toyouke-bime no Mikoto, Mizuhanome no Kami, Haniyasuhime no Mikoto, and Toyoukehime no Mikoto (four deities). {{Citation needed range|It is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the Engishiki-listed “Imaki Aoyasaka Inami Ikegami Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} {{ill|lt=Kami District|Q11634927|qid=Q11634927|1=Kami district}}, and its former shrine rank was village shrine.|date=February 2025}} === History === In {{ill|lt=Jinki era|Q1373210|qid=Q1373210|1=Jinki (era)}} Year 1 ({{ill|lt=724|Q23549|qid=Q23549|1=724}}),“Village Records of Kami District, Musashi Province,” Vol. 2, Sekiguchi Village the ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Abo clan|Q11450278|qid=Q11450278|1=Abo clan}} of the {{ill|lt=Tan Party|Q11368483|qid=Q11368483|1=Tan Party}}, one of the {{ill|lt=Musashi Seven Parties|Q11546323|qid=Q11546323|1=Musashi Seven Parties}}, transferred (kanjō) {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine|Q246370|qid=Q246370|1=Niukawakami Shrine (Q246370)}} of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, naming it “Niu Shrine.”Shrine noticeboard, “62nd Ise Grand Shrine Shikinen Sengu Commemoration: History of Imaki Aoyasaka Inami Ikegami Shrine” In {{ill|lt=Tenshō era|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} Year 5 ({{ill|lt=1577|Q6704|qid=Q6704|1=1577}}) March, the Sekiguchi area of Abo Village was separated to form Sekiguchi Village. At that time, this shrine was relocated from behind the Rokusho Shrine (present-day {{ill|lt=Abo Shrine (Kamikawa)|Q85884328|qid=Q85884328|lt=Abo Shrine|1=Abo Shrine}}) to Ikegami in Sekiguchi Village, becoming the guardian shrine of Sekiguchi Village.Saitama Prefecture, “Shrine Register” (compiled by Saitama Prefecture, compiled after 1879) The origin of the name “Sekiguchi” is said to derive from its location at the weir gate (sekiguchi) of the Abo irrigation canal serving the thirteen villages of the Abo territory. In the Meiji period, the shrine name was changed from “Niu Myōjin Shrine”“Newly Compiled Gazetteer of Musashi Province” (compiled by the Geography Bureau of {{ill|lt=Shōheizaka Academy|Q4059219|qid=Q4059219|1=Shoheizaka Academy}}, compiled between 1810 and 1830) to {{Reliability to be verified range|“Imaki Aoyasaka Inami Ikegami Shrine”|date=February 2025}}. '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} Year 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}) – Village shrine File:IwakiAosakaInamiIkegami-jinja(Kanna-machi) Torii.jpg|Torii gate File:IwakiAosakaInamiIkegami-jinja(Kanna-machi) Temizusha.jpg|Purification pavilion === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okami no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (おかみのかみ) * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime|Q2983724|qid=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime no Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} (とようけびめのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|qid=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome no Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} (みつはのめのかみ) * {{ill|lt=Haniyasu|Q9285919|qid=Q9285919|lt=Haniyasuhime no Mikoto|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime}} (はにやすひめのみこと) === Auxiliary shrines === Stone shrine group of five on the left * Kokage Shrine, Atago Shrine, Suwa Shrine, Yasaka Shrine Stone shrine group of four on the right * Hōsō Shrine, Inari Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenman Shrine File:IwakiAosakaInamiIkegami-jinja(Kanna-machi) Sarcophagus1.jpg|Kokage Shrine, Atago Shrine, Suwa Shrine, Yasaka Shrine File:IwakiAosakaInamiIkegami-jinja(Kanna-machi) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Hōsō Shrine, Inari Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Tenman Shrine === Events === * New Year Festival (January 2) * Spring Festival (March 19) * Kokage Festival (April 23) * Oshōjin (July 24) * Autumn Festival (October 19) * Niiname-sai (December 10) === Access === * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|qid=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}} {{ill|lt=Honjō Station|Q844906|qid=Q844906|1=Honjō Station (Saitama)}}, take the Asahi Bus bound for “Kanzen General Branch Office” for 20 minutes. Get off at “Tanjō Station Entrance.” 10 minutes on foot. * From JR {{ill|lt=Hachikō Line|Q1372071|qid=Q1372071|1=Hachikō Line}} {{ill|lt=Tanjō Station|Q845044|qid=Q845044|1=Tanshō Station}}, 11 minutes on foot (850 m) === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Imaki-Aosaka-Inami-Ikegami Shrine (Kamikawa, Saitama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Imaki-Aosaka-Inami-Ikegami Shrine (Kamikawa, Saitama)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Architecture of Kamikawa Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kamikawa]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]
